reasoning: The statistical trend tool provided a combined probability of 0.44 that the stock will exceed its current price in the near future, driven by seasonal analysis and flat recent momentum. We use this as our primary anchor for the short-term forecast (Dec 14). Entergy's Q4 dividend went ex-dividend in mid-November, so there is no immediate mechanical drop expected in December. However, an upcoming Q1 dividend ex-date will likely occur in mid-February, creating a minor headwind prior to the March 7 resolution date. Given the rule to increase uncertainty over longer horizons by moving estimates towards 0.5 over time, the probabilities are tapered from the short-term signal 0.45 up to 0.49 for the 3-month horizon.
